records.ety: [L. evincere vanquish completely, prevail, succeed in proving; e out + vincere to vanquish. See Victor, and cf. Evict.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Evinced (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Evincing (?).]

quotes: Error by his own arms is best evinced.

defs: To show in a clear manner; to prove beyond any reasonable doubt; to manifest; to make evident; to bring to light; to evidence.
quotes: Common sense and experience must and will evince the truth of this.
